---
title: "Knowledge Graph Navigation"
---

# Knowledge Graph Navigation

This resource supports both linear study and graph-based exploration.

The linear path acts like a road. The wiki acts like a map.

---

## How Backlinks Work

Every wiki page links to the pages it depends on and the pages that use it. Following these links reveals the conceptual structure of statistics as a connected graph — not a list of isolated topics.

**Example: Standard Error**

`[[standard-error]]` connects to:

- [confidence-intervals](wiki/concepts/confidence-intervals.md) — CI formula uses SE
- [hypothesis-testing-overview](wiki/concepts/hypothesis-testing-overview.md) — test statistic = (estimate − null) / SE
- [regression-slr](wiki/concepts/regression-slr.md) — β̂₁ and β̂₀ have their own SEs from MSE
- [control-charts](wiki/concepts/control-charts.md) — σ̂ from R̄/d₂ or s̄/c₄ plays the SE role

Once you understand SE as "standard deviation of the sampling distribution," every procedure that uses it becomes a variant of the same idea.

---

## Threshold Concepts

These are the constructs most worth deeply understanding first — once they click, multiple procedures unlock simultaneously.

| Concept | Why it's a threshold | Unlocks |
|---------|---------------------|---------|
| [Standard Error](wiki/concepts/standard-error.md) | Every CI and test statistic divides by one | CI, HT, regression, control charts |
| [SS Decomposition](wiki/concepts/ss-decomposition.md) | SS_T = SS_Model + SS_Error underlies every ANOVA table | Regression, CRD, RCBD, factorial, 2^k |
| [Sampling Distributions](wiki/concepts/distributions.md) | The link between a single sample and the population | Everything in inference |
| [Degrees of Freedom](wiki/concepts/degrees-of-freedom.md) | Which df goes with which test | Every t, χ², and F test |

---

## Cross-Section Connections

| Idea | Where it first appears | Where it recurs |
|------|----------------------|----------------|
| SE = σ/√n | Unit 1 CI | Regression βⱼ SE, control chart σ̂ |
| SS decomposition | Regression ANOVA | CRD, RCBD, factorial ANOVA |
| F-test | Regression F-test | One-way ANOVA, factorial ANOVA, 2^k |
| Residual diagnostics | Regression model adequacy | CRD and RCBD model checking |
| Variance estimation | σ² → MSE in regression | → MS_Error in ANOVA |

---

## Suggested Exploration Paths

**If you're confused about p-values:**
[hypothesis-testing-overview](wiki/concepts/hypothesis-testing-overview.md) → [which-test](wiki/concepts/which-test.md) → [reading-r-output](wiki/r-code/reading-r-output.md)

**If you're confused about ANOVA tables:**
[ss-decomposition](wiki/concepts/ss-decomposition.md) → [regression-ht](wiki/concepts/regression-ht.md) → [crd-one-way-anova](wiki/concepts/crd-one-way-anova.md) → [anova-table-examples](wiki/examples/anova-table-examples.md)

**If you're confused about which chart to use:**
[control-charts](wiki/concepts/control-charts.md) → [which-test-flowchart](wiki/reference/which-test-flowchart.md)

**If you're confused about 2^k designs:**
[ss-decomposition](wiki/concepts/ss-decomposition.md) → [factorial-anova](wiki/concepts/factorial-anova.md) → [2k-factorial-design](wiki/concepts/2k-factorial-design.md) → [2k-factorial-examples](wiki/examples/2k-factorial-examples.md)
